text: Left coronary angiogram revealed 90% obstruction of the left main due to dynamic compression by LVOT aneurysm.
Cardiopulmonary bypass was established with ascending aorta and right atrial candulation.
Right superior pulmonary vein went inserted.
Aorta was cross clamped, aortic root cardioplegia given.
Aorta was separated from the pulmonary artery.
You can see the aneurysm medially posterior to the aorta and laterally between the pulmonary artery and the lateral wall of LV.
Aortotomy was done.
Aneurysmal sac was seen below the left coronary cusp and you can see the sac filled with clots.
To increase the exposure, aorta was transected.
After which it became easier to analyze the anatomy and do the correction.
4 O-plugited pruline sutures were taken all around the edges of the aneurysm.
We took stitches carefully to avoid distortion of the left coronary cuspal attachment to the annulus.
A piece of bovine pericardium was used to close the aneurysmal defect.
Patch was then pushed down into the aorta and then sutures were tied off.
After placement of patch, the aortic valve was inspected.
Competency checked.
Aetotomy was closed in a routine fashion.
Since the patient had compression of left main, even though it was dynamic compression, we thought it will be safer to put a vein graft to left anterior descending artery.
We didn't want to use IMA because of the fear of competitive flow.
Patient was weaned off bypass easily.
He was discharged on POD6.
